explicit_text: I was born in Great Falls, Montana, on January 19, 1913. It was where my father, Lawrence Nelson, worked as a chef. My early years were spent there before moving to southern Montana after my mother passed away.

explicit_text: After my mother died, I was taken to Hysham, Montana, by relatives who were actually my second cousins. Hysham was the county seat of Treasure County, a wild place with cowboys and frequent shootings. I lived on a 160-acre ranch outside town and went to school there until the third grade.
